Vela Molecular Ridge.notes: Extensive molecular cloud complex in Vela at ~872 pc. One of the largest nearby star-forming regions.

Taurus-Perseus Cloud.notes: Extended dust cloud at ~356 pc between Taurus and Perseus. Part of the Gould Belt structure.

Vela Cloud.notes: Front face of the Vela Molecular Ridge at ~750 pc, associated with the Vela OB2 association.

Cassiopeia Cloud.notes: Molecular cloud complex in Cassiopeia at ~727 pc, associated with Cas OB2/OB5 on the near side of the Perseus Arm.

Cepheus-Cassiopeia Cloud.notes: Nearby molecular cloud at ~337 pc at the boundary of the Local Bubble, part of the Cepheus-Cassiopeia complex.

Camelopardalis Cloud.notes: Molecular cloud associated with the Cam OB1 association at ~918 pc at the outer edge of the Local Arm.

California Molecular Cloud.notes: Massive giant molecular cloud at ~446 pc rivaling Orion A in size. Discovered relatively recently despite its proximity.

Cepheus Flare.notes: Complex of molecular clouds at ~450 pc in Cepheus, site of low-mass star formation.

Taurus Molecular Cloud.notes: One of the nearest star-forming regions at ~184 pc. A classic low-mass star formation laboratory in Taurus-Auriga.

Cepheus OB3 Cloud.notes: Molecular cloud at ~770 pc associated with the Cep OB3 stellar association, an active region of sequential star formation.

Canis Major Cloud.notes: Molecular cloud complex at ~832 pc associated with the CMa OB1 association and CMa R1 reflection nebula.

Orion Molecular Cloud.notes: The nearest giant molecular cloud complex at ~500 pc. Contains the Orion Nebula (M42), Horsehead Nebula, and Barnard's Loop. Home to the Trapezium cluster and massive ongoing star formation, illuminated by hot O-type stars including Hatysa.

Cygnus Rift.notes: Nearest and most prominent section of the Great Rift, a massive lane of interstellar dust along the galactic plane. Foreground clouds at ~760 pc in the Cygnus direction.

Aquila Rift.notes: Near edge of the Aquila Rift dark cloud system at ~225 pc, one of the nearest major dust structures beyond the Local Bubble. Extends through Scutum, Aquila, Serpens, and Ophiuchus.

Cygnus X.notes: Near edge of one of the richest star-forming regions in the galaxy. Contains Cygnus OB2 (thousands of OB stars) and millions of solar masses of molecular gas.

Lupus Molecular Cloud.notes: One of the nearest low-mass star-forming regions at ~172 pc, part of the Scorpius-Centaurus association environment. Contains Lupus 1-6 subregions.

W40.notes: One of the closest sites of OB star formation at ~476 pc in the Serpens-Aquila Rift. Active massive star formation in a deeply embedded HII region.

Vulpecula Rift.notes: Continuation of the Aquila Rift dark cloud system through the Vulpecula-Sagitta region at ~545 pc. Part of the inner edge of the Local Arm dust lane.

Lacerta Cloud.notes: Molecular cloud associated with the Lacerta OB1 stellar association at ~543 pc, near the Cygnus-Lacerta boundary.
